question.text: Anita is 30 years old and wants to accumulate Rs.5 crore by age 60. If she expects an annual return of 12%, approximately how much should she invest monthly through SIP? (Use Rule of 72 for approximation)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Using financial calculator: FV = Rs.5 crore, n = 30 years (360 months), r = 12% p.a. (1% per month). The monthly SIP required is approximately Rs.14,300. Rule of 72 tells us money doubles every 6 years at 12%. **The Trap:** Rs.28,000 assumes roughly 8% returns. Rs.42,000 assumes much lower returns. Rs.7,000 underestimates the required amount. The power of compounding over 30 years is significant. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series X-A Chapter 2 covers TVM and SIP calculations. Series V-A tests SIP concepts for mutual fund distributors. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Show clients the difference that starting early makes — the same Rs.5 crore target at age 60 would require approximately Rs.44,000/month if starting at age 40 (only 20 years of compounding).
